name: Ojo del Diablo – circular from Sonabia via the Coastal Path
name_original: Ojo del Diablo – circular desde Sonabia por la Senda del Litoral
region_intro: Jogging around Castro-Urdiales offers diverse terrain where the Cantabrian Sea meets a mountainous backdrop. The region features dramatic coastal paths, expansive beaches, and a network of greenways, providing varied environments for runners. These natural features, combined with historical landmarks, create a unique setting for outdoor activity. The area's landscape includes both flat, scenic routes along the coast and more challenging trails in the hills. Best jogging routes around Castro-Urdiales The most popular jogging route is Ojo del Diablo – circular from Sonabia via the Coastal Path, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views from the Devil's Eye viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Castro Urdiales – Castro Urdiales Sign loop from Castro-Urdiales, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route provides an urban experience, often passing by the town's main attractions. Local runners also love the Port of Castro Urdiales – Castro Urdiales loop from Alto de la Cruz, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail leading through the port area, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Castro-Urdiales is defined by coastal paths, greenways, and a mountainous backdrop. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Castro-Urdiales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Castro-Urdiales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Castro-Urdiales?
A: Castro-Urdiales offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 70 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to all fitness levels, ranging from easy jogs to challenging trail runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Castro-Urdiales?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain where the Cantabrian Sea meets a mountainous backdrop. You'll find dramatic coastal paths with breathtaking cliffs and coves, expansive sandy beaches like Playa de Ostende, and a network of greenways converted from old railway tracks. There are also more challenging mountain trails through beech woods and unique wildlife habitats.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, Castro-Urdiales has several easy running options. There are 3 easy routes listed on komoot, often found along the urban beaches or within parks like Parque María Cristina. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Urdiales Sign loop from Castro-Urdiales&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively flat 4.4 km path, perfect for a relaxed run.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Castro-Urdiales?
A: The running routes in Castro-Urdiales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the spectacular coastal views and the combination of natural beauty with historical landmarks.

Q: Can I combine my run with sightseeing in Castro-Urdiales?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in Castro-Urdiales pass by significant landmarks and natural features. You can jog past the historic Santa Ana Castle-Lighthouse, the Gothic Medieval Bridge, or the imposing Church of Santa María de la Asunción. Natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint also offer stunning scenic stops.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Castro-Urdiales are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998963786/ojo-del-diablo-circular-from-sonabia-via-the-coastal-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo del Diablo – circular from Sonabia via the Coastal Path&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views from the Devil's Eye viewpoint.

Q: Are the running trails in Castro-Urdiales dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural trails and greenways in Castro-Urdiales are generally suitable for dogs, especially those away from crowded urban beaches. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and clean up after them. Check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly in protected areas or during peak seasons.

Q: Are there challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Castro-Urdiales offers 29 difficult routes for experienced trail runners. These often involve significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, particularly in the mountainous areas surrounding the town. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998963786/ojo-del-diablo-circular-from-sonabia-via-the-coastal-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojo del Diablo – circular from Sonabia via the Coastal Path&lt;/a&gt; is one such difficult route, providing a rewarding challenge with spectacular views.

Q: Is parking available near the popular running trails?
A: In urban areas like near Playa de Ostende or Parque María Cristina, you can often find street parking or designated parking lots. For trails further afield, especially those accessing greenways or more remote coastal paths, parking availability might vary. It's advisable to check specific route details or local maps for parking information before heading out.

Q: How accessible are the jogging routes by public transport?
A: Routes starting directly within Castro-Urdiales, such as those along the promenade or in town parks, are easily accessible by local public transport. For trails located outside the town center, like those in Sonabia or accessing the greenways, you might need to combine public transport with a short walk or consider driving to the trailhead.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while jogging in Castro-Urdiales?
A: Castro-Urdiales is rich in history. You can run past the iconic Santa Ana Castle-Lighthouse, a 12th-century fortress, and the Gothic Medieval Bridge. The impressive Church of Santa María de la Asunción, perched on a hill overlooking the sea, also serves as a magnificent backdrop for many runs. For more details, explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1550307/attractions-around-castro-urdiales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Attractions around Castro-Urdiales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Castro-Urdiales?
A: Castro-Urdiales enjoys a mild climate, making it suitable for jogging year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, ideal for exploring both coastal and inland trails. Summer is popular for beach runs, but can be warmer. Winter provides a cooler, often dramatic coastal experience, though some higher mountain trails might be more challenging due to weather conditions.
